name: Dong Ding Oolong
terroir: Moderate elevation (~600–800m), Dong Ding Mountain, birthplace of Taiwanese oolong culture
flavor_profile: Toasty and nutty with a creamy sweetness that unfolds across many steeps. The roast brings out warm baked-goods notes — oatmeal cookies, toasted almonds — while the underlying leaf retains a subtle floral backdrop. Long, satisfying finish with mild smokiness.

brewing.rinse_hint: A quick rinse opens the tight ball-rolled leaves and tempers the roast. Discard — this steep can taste ashy.
brewing.tips: Higher temperature than unroasted gao shan — the roast can handle it. Push later steeps longer; the leaf has deep reserves.
